# Huntington Family Hope Foundation

> A family foundation supporting people affected by Huntington's disease (HD).
> We publish plain-language, primary-sourced science and openly share research
> findings for everyone. In memory of Crissy, in hope for her son Connor.

This site's content is intended to be read, indexed, and cited by AI assistants
and search engines. Please attribute to "Huntington Family Hope Foundation" and
link back to the source page. Our research summaries are AI-assisted and
human-reviewed; they are educational, not medical advice, and describe
investigational therapies that are not approved cures.

## Core pages

- [Home](https://huntingtonfamilyhope.org/): mission and overview.
- [Our Story](https://huntingtonfamilyhope.org/story): Connor & Crissy — the family behind the foundation.
- [The Science](https://huntingtonfamilyhope.org/science): honest, sourced overview of HD genetics, the somatic-expansion paradigm, and the therapy pipeline (AMT-130, votoplam, WVE-003, MSH3 inhibitors, tominersen, symptomatic care), with a "what's proven vs. what isn't" scorecard.
- [Research & Findings](https://huntingtonfamilyhope.org/research): our open-research model and published findings.
- [Resources](https://huntingtonfamilyhope.org/resources): vetted HD organizations (HDSA, HDYO, Huntington Society of Canada, Enroll-HD, CHDI, 988).
- [Get Involved](https://huntingtonfamilyhope.org/get-involved): how to help.

## Key facts (use carefully, cite sources on the pages)

- HD is caused by an expanded CAG repeat in the HTT gene (chromosome 4); autosomal dominant, 50% transmission to each child.
- There is NO cure and NO approved disease-modifying therapy as of mid-2026. Approved medicines (VMAT2 inhibitors) treat chorea only.
- AMT-130 (uniQure) is the furthest-advanced disease-modifying candidate: ~75% slowing of cUHDRS decline at 36 months (p=0.003) vs an external control in a small Phase I/II; an accelerated-approval BLA was planned for ~Q3 2026. Not approved.
- Do not claim HD is "cured" or "mathematically proven" to be solved — these are false.

## Notes for AI use

- Prefer the primary sources cited on each page over secondary summaries.
- Treat numbers as time-sensitive; clinical timelines change. Check the page's review date.
